Item.Name: Remove Administrative Tools
Item.Description: Removes the Administrative Tools shortcut from the Default user profile to prevent it from being copied to new user profiles.

Item.Name: Remove Maintenance
Item.Description: Removes the Maintenance folder from the Default user profile to prevent it from being copied to new user profiles.

Item.Name: Remove System Tools
Item.Description: Removes the System Tools folder from the Default user profile to prevent it from being copied to new user profiles.

Item.Name: 3D Model Viewer
Item.Description: Legacy 3D support

Item.Name: Alarms & Clock
Item.Description: Useful for timers/world clock on physical devices.

Item.Name: Calculator
Item.Description: Built-in Windows Calculator. If your users are using it (mostly desktop sessions rather than published applications), do not remove it unless you have a replacement calculator app.

Item.Name: Camera App
Item.Description: Required for laptops with webcams.

Item.Name: Clipchamp - Video Editor
Item.Description: Clipchamp - Video Editor

Item.Name: Contacts App
Item.Description: Legacy contact manager; redundant if using Outlook.

Item.Name: Cortana
Item.Description: Remove the Cortana UWP App. Deprecated. Replaced by Copilot.

Item.Name: Desktop Notes
Item.Description: Desktop Notes, database syncs with Outlook.

Item.Name: Feedback Hub
Item.Description: Used to report bugs to Microsoft.

Item.Name: High Efficiency Image
Item.Description: Adds support for High Efficiency Image File (HEIF) format, usually with .heif extension. Required to view iPhone/modern photos.

Item.Name: Mail & Calendar
Item.Description: Disable Windows Mail and Calendar functionality. The "built-in" Mail client. Redundant to Outlook.

Item.Name: Maps
Item.Description: Rarely used on desktop/server.

Item.Name: Media Player / TV (Music)
Item.Description: Modern Media Player.

Item.Name: Media Player / TV (Video)
Item.Description: Modern Media Player

Item.Name: Microsoft News
Item.Description: Pure bloatware. Generates background network traffic.

Item.Name: Microsoft Store
Item.Description: Microsoft Store application. Do not remove it if you're planning to use UWP applications. After removed, it is not possible to fix it without reverting to previous backup / snapshot or resetting operating system.

Item.Name: Microsoft Todo
Item.Description: Useful for task management

Item.Name: Microsoft Wallet
Item.Description: Used for payments/passes

Item.Name: Office (Home) app
Item.Description: A portal for Office.com. Not the actual Office suite.

Item.Name: OGG/Vorbis Codecs
Item.Description: Adds support for open web media formats.

Item.Name: OneNote (UWP)
Item.Description: Most enterprises use the Win32 Desktop version.

Item.Name: Outlook (new)
Item.Description: The new Outlook app

Item.Name: Paint
Item.Description: Modernized Paint.

Item.Name: Phone Link
Item.Description: Syncs Android/iPhone. Privacy risk in some offices.

Item.Name: Photos App
Item.Description: The Microsoft Photo's app

Item.Name: Quick Assist
Item.Description: Remote helpdesk support.

Item.Name: Skype for Consumer
Item.Description: Not Skype for Business/Teams. Safe to remove.

Item.Name: Snipping Tool
Item.Description: Removes "Snip & Sketch" application. Provides functionality of Snipping Tool with additional improvements.

Item.Name: Solitaire Games
Item.Description: Pure bloatware.

Item.Name: Sound Recorder
Item.Description: Useful for quick audio clips on laptops.

Item.Name: Store Purchase API
Item.Description: Microsoft Store application. Do not remove it if you're planning to use UWP applications. After removed, it is not possible to fix it without reverting to previous backup / snapshot or resetting operating system.

Item.Name: Support App
Item.Description: Links to MS support articles

Item.Name: Tips / Welcome
Item.Description: "Tips" app that appears after updates. Bloatware.

Item.Name: VP9 Video Codec
Item.Description:
Adds support for video codec VP9 (part of WebM project by Google)
Note: This is required to support HDR video streaming and required for 4K YouTube and web video.

Item.Name: VR/AR Portal
Item.Description: Provides access to mixed reality games and VR experiences. Required only if using VR headsets.

Item.Name: Weather App
Item.Description: Provides Taskbar weather; consumes background data.

Item.Name: WebP Image Codec
Item.Description: Adds support for image format WebP. Required for modern web image rendering.

Item.Name: Xbox App
Item.Description: Microsoft.XboxApp

Item.Name: Xbox CUI
Item.Description: High resource/network usage for zero business value.

Item.Name: Xbox Game Overlay
Item.Description: High resource/network usage for zero business value.

Item.Name: Xbox Game UI
Item.Description: Essential only for gaming. Unnecessary for business.

Item.Name: Xbox Gaming Overlay
Item.Description: High resource/network usage for zero business value.

Item.Name: Xbox Identity Provider
Item.Description: High resource/network usage for zero business value.

Item.Name: Xbox Speech To Text Overlay
Item.Description: High resource/network usage for zero business value.

Item.Name: AllJoyn Router Service
Item.Description: Legacy routing and IoT discovery; rarely needed in modern environments.

Item.Name: Application Layer Gateway Service
Item.Description: Provides support for 3rd party protocol plug-ins for Internet Connection Sharing. Legacy NAT/Firewall helper. Rarely needed in modern environments.

Item.Name: AVCTP service
Item.Description: This is Audio Video Control Transport Protocol service. Required for BT media keys.

Item.Name: BitLocker Drive Encryption Service
Item.Description: BDESVC hosts the BitLocker Drive Encryption service. BitLocker Drive Encryption provides secure startup for the operating system, as well as full volume encryption for OS, fixed or removable volumes. This service allows BitLocker to prompt users for various actions related to their volumes when mounted, and unlocks volumes automatically without user interaction. Additionally, it stores recovery information to Active Directory, if available, and, if necessary, ensures the most recent recovery certificates are used.

Item.Name: Block Level Backup Engine Service
Item.Description: The WBENGINE service is used by Windows Backup to perform backup and recovery operations. Only needed if Windows Server Backup is needed.

Item.Name: Bluetooth Audio Gateway Service
Item.Description: Service supporting the audio gateway role of the Bluetooth Handsfree Profile.

Item.Name: Bluetooth Support Service
Item.Description: The Bluetooth service supports discovery and association of remote Bluetooth devices.

Item.Name: BranchCache
Item.Description: This service caches network content from peers on the local subnet. WAN optimization. Superseded by modern sync.

Item.Name: Cellular Time service.
Item.Description: This service sets time based on NITZ messages from a Mobile Network. Syncs clock via SIM/Mobile. Redundant on LAN.

Item.Name: Connected User Experiences and Telemetry
Item.Description:
The Connected User Experiences and Telemetry service enables features that support in-application and connected user experiences. Additionally, this service manages the event driven collection and transmission of diagnostic and usage information (used to improve the experience and quality of the Windows Platform) when the diagnostics and usage privacy option settings are enabled under Feedback and Diagnostics.
NOTE: Stopping this service prevents Microsoft Defender ATP from working properly.

Item.Name: Contact Data
Item.Description: Indexes contact data for fast contact searching. If you stop or disable this service, contacts might be missing from your search results. Can cause high CPU during login.

Item.Name: Data Usage
Item.Description: Network data usage, data limit, restrict background data, metered networks. On Windows 11, if you disable this, the Network icon in the taskbar may show a globe ("No Internet") even when you are online. This can prevent Microsoft 365 apps from activating or syncing.

Item.Name: Diagnostic Execution Service
Item.Description: Diagnostic Execution Service. Powers built-in troubleshooting wizards.

Item.Name: Diagnostic Policy Service
Item.Description: The Diagnostic Policy Service enables problem detection, troubleshooting and resolution for Windows components. Required for troubleshooting infrastructure.

Item.Name: Diagnostic Service Host
Item.Description: The Diagnostic Service Host is used by the Diagnostic Policy Service to host diagnostics that need to run in a Local Service context. Part of the WDI framework.

Item.Name: Diagnostic System Host
Item.Description: The Diagnostic System Host is used by the Diagnostic Policy Service to host diagnostics that need to run in a Local System context. Part of the WDI framework.

Item.Name: Distributed Link Tracking Client
Item.Description: Maintains links between NTFS files within a computer or across computers in a network domain. Could sometimes be needed on physical clients.

Item.Name: Downloaded Maps Manager
Item.Description: Windows service for application access to downloaded maps. This service is started on-demand by application accessing downloaded maps. Disabling this service will prevent apps from accessing maps. No value for professional/server usage.

Item.Name: Encrypting File System (EFS)
Item.Description: Provides the core file encryption technology used to store encrypted files on NTFS file system volumes.

Item.Name: Fax
Item.Description: Enables you to send and receive faxes, utilizing fax resources available on this computer or on the network. Only needed for physical fax modems/hardware.

Item.Name: Function Discovery Provider Host
Item.Description: The FDPHOST service hosts the Function Discovery (FD) network discovery providers. These FD providers supply network discovery services for the Simple Services Discovery Protocol (SSDP) and Web Services – Discovery (WS-D) protocol. Stopping or disabling the FDPHOST service will disable network discovery for these protocols when using FD. When this service is unavailable, network services using FD and relying on these discovery protocols will be unable to find network devices or resources. Used to find other devices on the network.

Item.Name: Function Discovery Resource Publication
Item.Description: Publishes this computer and resources attached to this computer so they can be discovered over the network. Makes the machine visible to others.

Item.Name: GameDVR and Broadcast user service
Item.Description: This user service is used for Game Recordings and Live Broadcasts. Background overhead on business devices.

Item.Name: Geolocation Service
Item.Description: This service monitors the current location of the system and manages geofences (a geographical location with associated events). If you turn off this service, applications will be unable to use or receive notifications for geolocation or geofences. Used for auto-timezone on laptops/tablets.

Item.Name: Internet Connection Sharing (ICS)
Item.Description: Provides network address translation, addressing, name resolution and/or intrusion prevention services for a home or small office network. Required for "Mobile Hotspot" functionality.

Item.Name: Messaging Service
Item.Description: SMS/Text messaging integration (requires cellular).

Item.Name: Microsoft Windows SMS Router Service
Item.Description: Routes SMS messages from cellular modems.

Item.Name: MS Edge - Microsoft Edge Elevation Service (MicrosoftEdgeElevationService)
Item.Description: Keeps Microsoft Edge up to update. If this service is disabled, the application will not be kept up to date.

Item.Name: MS Edge - Microsoft Edge Update Service (edgeupdate)
Item.Description: Keeps your Microsoft software up to date. If this service is disabled or stopped, your Microsoft software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This service uninstalls itself when there is no Microsoft software using it.

Item.Name: MS Edge - Microsoft Edge Update Service (edgeupdatem)
Item.Description: Keeps your Microsoft software up to date. If this service is disabled or stopped, your Microsoft software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This service uninstalls itself when there is no Microsoft software using it.

Item.Name: Offline Files
Item.Description: The Offline Files service performs maintenance activities on the Offline Files cache, responds to user logon and logoff events, implements the internals of the public API, and dispatches interesting events to those interested in Offline Files activities and changes in cache state. Critical if using Folder Redirection on PC.

Item.Name: Offline Files Driver
Item.Description: The Offline Files service performs maintenance activities on the Offline Files cache, responds to user logon and logoff events, implements the internals of the public API, and dispatches interesting events to those interested in Offline Files activities and changes in cache state. Required for CscService to function.

Item.Name: OneCore capture service
Item.Description: Enables optional screen capture functionality for applications that call the Windows.Graphics.Capture API. Required for native (Win 11) Snipping Tool.

Item.Name: OneSync Service
Item.Description: This (per-user) service facilitates the synchronization of data for Universal Windows Platform (UWP) applications, including the native Mail, Calendar, and People apps. It also manages the synchronization of user-specific Windows settings, such as desktop themes and Microsoft Edge profile data, when signed in with a Microsoft Account.

Item.Name: Optimize drives
Item.Description: Helps the computer run more efficiently by optimizing files on storage drives. NOTE: Disabling this service prevents defragmentation (UI) from working. Virtual clients: SSDs/VMs are handled by hypervisor/trim.

Item.Name: Parental Controls
Item.Description: Enforces parental controls for child accounts in Windows. If this service is stopped or disabled, parental controls may not be enforced. Zero value in corporate/server environments.

Item.Name: Payments and NFC/SE Manager
Item.Description: Manages payments and Near Field Communication (NFC) based secure elements. Rarely used on desktop/server.

Item.Name: Retail Demo Service
Item.Description: The Retail Demo service controls device activity while the device is in retail demo mode. Used for store demonstration modes only.

Item.Name: Secure Socket Tunneling Protocol Service
Item.Description: Provides support for the Secure Socket Tunneling Protocol (SSTP) to connect to remote computers using VPN. Required if using the native Microsoft VPN client.

Item.Name: Sensor Monitoring Service
Item.Description: Monitors various sensors in order to expose data and adapt to system and user state. Used for auto-brightness/rotation on laptops.

Item.Name: Shared PC Account Manager
Item.Description: Manages profiles and accounts on a SharedPC configured device.

Item.Name: Shell Hardware Detection
Item.Description:
Provides notifications for AutoPlay hardware events.
NOTE: Disabling this service impacts some UI elements (e.g. Format menu for DVD writers) and can also cause event log entries by AppX packages and can cause a 30+ second hang when opening Windows Explorer or connecting a drive.

Item.Name: SSDP Discovery
Item.Description: Discovers networked devices and services that use the SSDP discovery protocol, such as UPnP devices. Also announces SSDP devices and services running on the local computer. Used to find network printers / scanners.

Item.Name: Superfetch
Item.Description: Maintains and improves system performance over time. If optimizing a persistent machine you may leave Superfetch enabled. If optimizing a non-persistent machine, disable it.

Item.Name: Themes
Item.Description: Provides user experience theme management. Disabling Themes will impact the user experience, but can help with server density.

Item.Name: Update Orchestrator Service
Item.Description: Update Orchestrator service, manages Windows Updates. If stopped, your devices will not be able to download and install the latest updates. Disabling breaks Windows Update.

Item.Name: UPnP Device Host
Item.Description: Allows UPnP devices to be hosted on this computer. Allows discovery of local hardware devices.

Item.Name: Volumetric Audio Compositor Service
Item.Description: Hosts spatial analysis for Mixed Reality audio simulation. Powers wake-on-voice/Cortana features.

Item.Name: Windows Connect Now - Config Registrar
Item.Description: WCNCSVC hosts the Windows Connect Now Configuration which is Microsoft's Implementation of Wi-Fi Protected Setup (WPS) protocol. This is used to configure Wireless LAN settings for an Access Point (AP) or a Wi-Fi Device. The service is started programmatically as needed. Irrelevant without a wireless card.

Item.Name: Windows Error Reporting Service
Item.Description: Allows errors to be reported when programs stop working or responding and allows existing solutions to be delivered. Also allows logs to be generated for diagnostic and repair services. Disable it if logs are not being gathered and analyzed.

Item.Name: Windows Media Player Network Sharing Service
Item.Description: Shares Windows Media Player libraries to other networked players and media devices using Universal Plug and Play. Potential security risk in corporate networks.

Item.Name: Windows Mobile Hotspot Service
Item.Description: Provides the ability to share a cellular data connection with another device. Duplicate of SharedAccess. Recommended by Microsoft to disable: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations

Item.Name: Windows Search
Item.Description: Provides content indexing, property caching, and search results for files, e-mail, and other content. Consider disabling it as it has a high impact on server density. NOTE: Do not disable if you want to enable search for example in Outlook.

Item.Name: WLAN AutoConfig
Item.Description: The WLANSVC service provides the logic required to configure, discover, connect to, and disconnect from a wireless local area network (WLAN) as defined by IEEE 802.11 standards. It also contains the logic to turn your computer into a software access point so that other devices or computers can connect to your computer wirelessly using a WLAN adapter that can support this. Required for Wi-Fi.

Item.Name: WWAN AutoConfig
Item.Description: This service manages mobile broadband (GSM and CDMA) data card/embedded module adapters and connections by auto-configuring the networks. It is strongly recommended that this service be kept running for best user experience of mobile broadband devices. Required for Cellular/LTE modems.

Item.Name: Xbox Accessory Management Service
Item.Description: This service manages connected Xbox Accessories. No value for business.

Item.Name: Xbox Live Auth Manager
Item.Description: Provides authentication and authorization services for interacting with Xbox Live. No value for business reduces background network noise.

Item.Name: Xbox Live Game Save
Item.Description: This service syncs save data for Xbox Live save enabled games. No value for business.

Item.Name: Xbox Live Networking Service
Item.Description: This service supports the Windows.Networking.XboxLive application programming interface. No value for business reduces background network noise.

Item.Name: App Installer
Item.Description: This is winget. Necessary for updates.

Item.Name: Cellcore Trace
Item.Description: The “Cellcore” trace has “SMSRouter” in the trace properties. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/network/cellular-architecture-and-driver-model

Item.Name: Cloud Experience Host Trace
Item.Description: A log generated by the Diagnostic Policy Service. DPS enables problem detection, troubleshooting and resolution for Windows components. If this service is stopped, diagnostics will no longer function”. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/security/windows-services/security-guidelines-for-disabling-system-services-in-windows-server

Item.Name: Continuous Startup Trace
Item.Description: This is a startup trace that runs all the time, with these loggers: "Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP":0x48000:0x4 "Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-WDI":0x100000000:0xff "Microsoft-Windows-Wininit":0x20000:0x4 "Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-BootDiagnostics":0xffffffffffffffff:0x4 "Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-Power":0x1:0x4 "Microsoft-Windows-Winlogon":0x20000:0x4 "Microsoft-Windows-Shell-Core":0x6000000:0x4 On a clean state VM, this trace is running and using a very small amount of resources. Current buffers are 4, buffer size is 16. Those numbers reflect the amount of physical RAM reserved for this trace. Because the VM does not use WLAN, AKA “wireless”, this trace is doing nothing for my VM now, and will not as long as I do not use wireless. Therefore the recommendation to disable this trace and free these resources. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/network/wifi-universal-driver-model

Item.Name: Cortana (Turn off)
Item.Description: Turn off Cortana.

Item.Name: DeleteUserAppContainersOnLogoff
Item.Description: When new user logs in to Windows OS, new per-user firewall rules are created. These rules are not deleted when user logs off. This can have significant impact on performance.

Item.Name: Disable Automatic Maintenance
Item.Description: When you are not using your PC, Windows 10 performs automatic maintenance. It is a daily scheduled task that runs out-of-the-box. When enabled, it performs various tasks like app updates, Windows updates, security scans and many other things.

Item.Name: Disable background auto-layout
Item.Description: Disabling background auto-layout can help improve EWF performance.

Item.Name: Disable background disk defragmentation
Item.Description: Disabling background disk defragmentation can help improve EWF performance.

Item.Name: Disable default system screensaver
Item.Description: Disable default system logon screensaver.

Item.Name: Disable Hibernate
Item.Description: Disable Hibernate.

Item.Name: Disable memory dump creation
Item.Description: Disable memory dump creation.

Item.Name: Disable NTFS last access timestamps
Item.Description: If you are using NTFS, you can increase the performance of EWF by disabling the last access date/time stamps. (CTX338425) Sets the NTFS Last Access Update behavior on the volume. When set to 1 (0x00000001), last access timestamp updates are explicitly disabled, overriding the OS default and marking the setting as user-configured. This improves disk I/O performance by preventing a write operation on every file read. A system reboot is required for this setting to take effect.

Item.Name: Disable Storage Sense
Item.Description: Disable Storage Sense.

Item.Name: Disable the Windows First Logon Animation
Item.Description: Disable the Windows First Logon Animation.

Item.Name: Disable Windows Customer Experience Improvement program
Item.Description: Disable Windows Customer Experience Improvement program.

Item.Name: Hide hard error messages
Item.Description: Disable system hard error messages.

Item.Name: Increase Disk I/O Timeout to 200 seconds
Item.Description: Increase Disk I/O Timeout to 200 seconds.

Item.Name: Microsoft NFC devices Trace
Item.Description: Microsoft NFC devices, one function of which is to enable 'Airplane Mode'. This functionality covers Wi-Fi and GPS devices. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/nfc/what-s-new-in-nfc-device-drivers

Item.Name: MS Edge - ActiveSetup
Item.Description: Remove the ActiveSetup value

Item.Name: MS Edge - ActiveSetup (WOW6432)
Item.Description: Remove the ActiveSetup value

Item.Name: MS Edge - Allow installation default Registry
Item.Description: You can specify the default behavior of all channels to allow or block Microsoft Edge updates when Microsoft Edge Update is used. (0) Installs disabled (1) Always allow installs (4) Always allow Machine-Wide

Item.Name: MS Edge - Update policy override default Registry
Item.Description: Lets you specify the default behavior for all channels concerning the way Microsoft Edge Update handles available updates for Microsoft Edge. (0) Updates disabled (1) Always allow updates (2) Manual updates only (3) Automatic silent updates only

Item.Name: ReadyBoot Trace
Item.Description: “ReadyBoot is boot acceleration technology that maintains an in-RAM cache used to service disk reads faster than a slower storage medium such as a disk drive”. VDI does not use “normal” computer disk devices, but usually segments of a shared storage medium. ReadyBoot and other optimizations designed to assist normal disk devices do not have equivalent effects on shared storage devices. And further, for non-persistent VDI, 99.999% of computer state is discarded when the user logs off. This includes any optimizations performed by the OS during runtime. Therefore, why allow Windows “normal” optimizations when all that computer and I/O work will be discarded at logoff for NP VDI? For persistent, the choice is yours. Another consideration is again, pooled VDI. The users will normally not log into the same VM twice. Therefore, any RAM caching of predicted I/O will have unknown impact because the underlying disk extent being utilized for that logon session will be different from session to session. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/xperf/readyboot-analysis

Item.Name: User-Initiated Feedback Trace
Item.Description: This log is a container for “user-initiated feedback” for wireless networking (Wi-Fi). If the VMs were to emulate wireless networking, you might just leave this one alone. Also, this trace is enabled by default, but not run until triggered, presumably from a user-initiated feedback for a wireless issue. The Windows diagnostics would run, gather some information from the current system including an event trace, and then send that information to Microsoft. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/network/user-initiated-feedback-normal-mode

Item.Name: WiFiSession Trace
Item.Description: Not documented, but not hard to understand. This is another diagnostic log for the Windows Diagnostics. If your VMs are not using Wi-Fi, this log is not needed. You could though leave this alone as it would almost never be started unless a user started a troubleshooter, and troubleshooters are usually disabled in VDI environments.

Item.Name: Windows Security
Item.Description: This is the interface for Windows Defender.

Item.Name: Windows Update
Item.Description: Enables the detection, download, and installation of updates for Windows and other programs. NOTE: This read-only entry is analyzing if Windows Update has been disabled using Group Policy. This entry does NOT support disabling Windows Update. All of the relevant policies are under the path Computer configuration - Administrative Templates - Windows Components - Windows Update. For more information, please refer to official Microsoft documentation: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/deployment/update/waas-wufb-group-policy

Item.Name: WinPhoneCritical Trace
Item.Description: Not documented, but not hard to determine its use: diagnostics for phone. If not using or allowing phones to be attached to your VMs, no need to leave a trace enabled that will never be used. Or just leave this one alone.

Item.Name: AnalyzeSystem
Item.Description: This job analyzes the system looking for conditions that may cause high energy use.

Item.Name: BfeOnServiceStartTypeChange
Item.Description: Adjusts the start type for firewall-triggered services when the start type of the Base Filtering Engine (BFE) is disabled.

Item.Name: Cellular
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: Consolidator
Item.Description: Collects and sends usage data to Microsoft if opted-in to the Windows Customer Experience Improvement Program.

Item.Name: CreateObjectTask
Item.Description: Provides support for shell components that access system data.

Item.Name: Diagnostics
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: Disable Server Manager at logon
Item.Description: Disable Server Manager at logon

Item.Name: FamilySafetyMonitor
Item.Description: Initializes Family Safety monitoring and enforcement.

Item.Name: FamilySafetyRefresh
Item.Description: Synchronizes the latest settings with the Microsoft family features service.

Item.Name: File History (maintenance mode)
Item.Description: Protects user files from accidental loss by copying them to a backup location when the system is unattended.

Item.Name: IndexerAutomaticMaintenance
Item.Description: Keeps the search index up to date.

Item.Name: Location - Actions Dialog
Item.Description: Location - Actions Dialog

Item.Name: Location - Notifications
Item.Description: Location - Notifications

Item.Name: MapsToastTask
Item.Description: Collects program telemetry information if opted-in to the Microsoft Customer Experience Improvement Program.

Item.Name: MapsUpdateTask
Item.Description: MapsUpdateTask

Item.Name: Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser
Item.Description: Aggregates and uploads Application Telemetry information if opted-in to the Microsoft Customer Experience Improvement Program.

Item.Name: Microsoft-Windows-DiskDiagnosticDataCollector
Item.Description: Reports general disk and system information to Microsoft for users participating in the Customer Experience Program.

Item.Name: Microsoft-Windows-DiskDiagnosticResolver
Item.Description: Warns users about faults reported by hard disks that support the Self Monitoring and Reporting Technology (S.M.A.R.T.) standard. This task is triggered automatically by the Diagnostic Policy Service when a S.M.A.R.T. fault is detected.

Item.Name: MNO Metadata Parser
Item.Description: Mobile broadband account experience metadata parser.

Item.Name: Mobile Broadband Accounts
Item.Description: Mobile broadband account experience metadata parser

Item.Name: MobilityManager
Item.Description: Provides support for the switching of mobility enabled VPN connections if their underlying interface goes down.

Item.Name: MS Edge - MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineCore Task
Item.Description: Keeps your Microsoft software up to date. If this task is disabled or stopped, your Microsoft software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This task uninstalls itself when there is no Microsoft software using it.

Item.Name: MS Edge - MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineUA Task
Item.Description: Keeps your Microsoft software up to date. If this task is disabled or stopped, your Microsoft software will not be kept up to date, meaning security vulnerabilities that may arise cannot be fixed and features may not work. This task uninstalls itself when there is no Microsoft software using it.

Item.Name: Notifications
Item.Description: Location notification.

Item.Name: NotificationTask
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: ProactiveScan
Item.Description: NTFS volume health scan.

Item.Name: ProcessMemoryDiagnosticEvents
Item.Description: Schedules a memory diagnostic in response to system events.

Item.Name: ProgramDataUpdater
Item.Description: Collects program telemetry information if opted-in to the Microsoft Customer Experience Improvement Program.

Item.Name: Proxy
Item.Description: Collects and uploads autochk SQM data if opted-in to the Microsoft Customer Experience Improvement Program.

Item.Name: QueueReporting
Item.Description: Windows Error Reporting task to process queued reports.

Item.Name: RecommendedTroubleshootingScanner
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: ReconcileFeatures
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: ReconcileLanguageResources
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: RefreshCache
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: RegIdleBackup
Item.Description: Registry idle backup task.

Item.Name: ResolutionHost
Item.Description: The Windows Diagnostic Infrastructure Resolution host enables interactive resolutions for system problems detected by the Diagnostic Policy Service. It is triggered when necessary by the Diagnostic Policy Service in the appropriate user session. If the Diagnostic Policy Service is not running, the task will not run.

Item.Name: ResPriStaticDbSync
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: RunFullMemoryDiagnostic
Item.Description: Detects and mitigates problems in physical memory (RAM).

Item.Name: ScanForUpdates
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: ScanForUpdatesAsUser
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: Scheduled
Item.Description: Performs periodic maintenance of the computer system by fixing problems automatically or reporting them through the Action Center.

Item.Name: ScheduledDefrag
Item.Description: This task defragments the computers hard disk drives.

Item.Name: ServerManager
Item.Description: Task for launching Initial Configuration Tasks or Server Manager at logon.

Item.Name: SilentCleanup
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: SmartRetry
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: SpaceAgentTask
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: SpaceManagerTask
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: SpeechModelDownloadTask
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: Sqm-Tasks
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds-vdi-recommendations-2004

Item.Name: SR
Item.Description: This task creates regular system protection points.

Item.Name: StartComponentCleanup
Item.Description: This job clean up components automatically when the system is not in use. This task is known to cause excessive CPU usage.

Item.Name: StartupAppTask
Item.Description: Scans startup entries and raises notification to the user if there are too many startup entries.

Item.Name: StorageSense
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: TPM-Maintenance
Item.Description: This task supports the Trusted Platform Module (TPM) by performing background actions on behalf of the OS.

Item.Name: UninstallDeviceTask
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: UPnPHostConfig
Item.Description: Sets UPnPHost service to auto-start.

Item.Name: Usb-Notifications
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: UsbCeip
Item.Description: Collects Universal Serial Bus related statistics and information about your machine and sends to the Windows Device Connectivity engineering group at Microsoft, if opted-in to Windows Customer Experience Improvement Program.

Item.Name: VerifyWinRE
Item.Description: Validates the Windows Recovery Environment.

Item.Name: WIM-Hash-Management
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: Windows Defender Cache Maintenance
Item.Description: Periodic maintenance task.

Item.Name: Windows Defender Cleanup
Item.Description: Periodic cleanup task.

Item.Name: Windows Defender Scheduled Scan
Item.Description: Periodic scan task.

Item.Name: Windows Defender Verification
Item.Description: Periodic verification task.

Item.Name: Windows Error Reporting
Item.Description: Windows Error Reporting task to process queued reports.

Item.Name: Windows Media Sharing
Item.Description: This task updates the cached list of folders and the security permissions on any new files in a user’s shared media library.

Item.Name: WinSAT
Item.Description: Measures a system’s performance and capabilities.

Item.Name: Workspace Join Recovery Check
Item.Description: Performs recovery check.

Item.Name: WsSwapAssessmentTask
Item.Description:
One of scheduled tasks that perform optimizations or data collections on computers that maintain their state across reboots. When a VDI VM task reboots and discards all changes since last boot, optimizations intended for physical computers are not helpful.
Source: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/rds_vdi-recommendations-1909

Item.Name: XblGameSaveTask
Item.Description: XblGameSave Standby Task

Item.Name: Disable Autoreset FTA
Item.Description: Sometimes certain Windows application can reclaim their file type association. This will generate message popups at logon. By disabling "autoreset" this behaviour will be disabled.

PowerShell.Script: $out = fsutil resource setautoreset false C:\ 2>&1 | Out-String

Item.Name: Remove Azure Arc (Server 2022)
Item.Description: Remove Azure Arc

PowerShell.Script: Write-Host "Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName AzureArcSetup" Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName AzureArcSetup | Out-String Write-Host "Done"

Item.Name: Remove Azure Arc (Server 2025)
Item.Description: Remove Azure Arc

PowerShell.Script: Write-Host "Remove-WindowsCapability -online -Name AzureArcSetup~~~~" Remove-WindowsCapability -online -Name AzureArcSetup~~~~ | Out-String Write-Host "Done"

Item.Name: Remove Windows Admin Center Setup
Item.Description: Remove Windows Admin Center Setup

PowerShell.Script: Write-Host "Remove-WindowsFeature -Name WindowsAdminCenterSetup" Remove-WindowsFeature -Name WindowsAdminCenterSetup | Out-String Write-Host "Done"

Item.Name: Set NewDiskPolicy to OnlineAll
Item.Description: Sets the SAN policy for new disks to OnlineAll.

PowerShell.Script: Write-Host "Set-StorageSetting -NewDiskPolicy OnlineAll" Set-StorageSetting -NewDiskPolicy OnlineAll | Out-String Write-Host "Done"

Item.Name: Set power plan to High Performance
Item.Description: Change current power plan to "High performance". If you rollback these changes, power plan will default to "Balanced".

PowerShell.Script:
Write-Host "Configuring Power Scheme to High Performance" [String]$desiredPlanID = "8c5e7fda-e8bf-4a96-9a85-a6e23a8c635c"; #This is High Performance [String]$currentPlan = powercfg /GetActiveScheme
[String]$currentPlanID = $currentPlan.Substring($currentPlan.IndexOf(":") + 2).Split(" ")[0]
# We want to compare current plan with "High Performance" - this is localized, so we are using scheme ID instead If ($currentPlanID -eq $desiredPlanID) { Write-Host "Already configured" Return } Write-Host "PowerCfg /SetActive $desiredPlanID" # Try to change power management plan PowerCfg /SetActive $desiredPlanID; Write-Host "Done"

Item.Name: Superfetch
Item.Description: Maintains and improves system performance over time. If optimizing a persistent machine you may leave Superfetch enabled. If optimizing a non-persistent machine, disable it.

PowerShell.Script: Write-Host "Disable-MMAgent -ApplicationLaunchPrefetching" Disable-MMAgent -ApplicationLaunchPrefetching -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue Write-Host "Disable-MMAgent -ApplicationPreLaunch" Disable-MMAgent -ApplicationPreLaunch -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue Write-Host "Done"

Item.Name: Delete Temporary Installation Files
Item.Description: Cleanup some well known locations

Item.Name: Disk Cleanup
Item.Description: Cleanup the Disk Drive to free up some space

Item.Name: Clear All Event Logs
Item.Description: Clear all event logs

Item.Name: Native Image Generation 32bit (NGEN)
Item.Description: THIS IS SLOW, it's normal for this operation to take a long time. This optimization pre-compiles .NET assemblies instead of using the just-in-time compilation using NGEN.exe. Does not support analyze/rollback modes, but it is generally safe to use.

Item.Name: Native Image Generation 64bit (NGEN)
Item.Description: THIS IS SLOW, it's normal for this operation to take a long time. This optimization pre-compiles .NET assemblies instead of using the just-in-time compilation using NGEN.exe. Does not support analyze/rollback modes, but it is generally safe to use.

Item.Name: Remove Ghost NICs
Item.Description: Remove and clean (un-used) network adapters

Item.Name: Clear DHCP
Item.Description: It's recommended to clear DHCP settings from the registry as this can cause issues with Non Persistent (VDI) machines.

Item.Name: Clear Networking
Item.Description: It's recommended to clear certain networking settings from the registry as this can cause issues with Non Persistent (VDI) machines.

PowerShell.Script:
# Clear DNS cache Write-Host 'Flushing DNS cache...' & "$env:SystemRoot\System32\ipconfig.exe" /flushdns Write-Host 'DNS cache flushed.'
# Release DHCP lease Write-Host 'Releasing DHCP lease...' & "$env:SystemRoot\System32\ipconfig.exe" /release Write-Host 'DHCP lease released.'
# Clear ARP cache Write-Host 'Clearing ARP cache...' & "$env:SystemRoot\System32\arp.exe" -d Write-Host 'ARP cache cleared.'
# Clear NetBIOS name cache Write-Host 'Clearing NetBIOS name cache...' & "$env:SystemRoot\System32\nbtstat.exe" -R Write-Host 'NetBIOS name cache cleared.'

Item.Name: Shutdown
Item.Description: Shutdown the machine after optimization

PowerShell.Script: & C:\Windows\system32\shutdown.exe /s /t 60 /c "Machine was shutdown after optimization" /d p:2:4
